description_long: This object consists of four vintage-style metal chairs, each mounted on a square base, with a utilitarian design reminiscent of old school or public seating. The chairs feature a tubular metal frame painted in a faded green, with flat, rectangular seat and backrest panels, some of which show signs of wear and rust. One chair has an attached wooden writing tablet on the right side, while another has a metal armrest or support on the left. The bases are dark and slightly raised, likely to stabilize the chairs for display or use in a fixed setting. The overall appearance suggests durability and a design intended for heavy use in public or semi-public urban environments. The surfaces are mostly hard and smooth, with some matte and worn areas indicating age and frequent use.
description: A set of four vintage green metal chairs with flat seats and backrests, some featuring attached writing tablets or armrests, all mounted on square bases. The design is utilitarian and shows signs of wear, suitable for public or urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four chairs, highlighting the seat and backrest panels, with one chair featuring a wooden writing tablet and another with a metal armrest.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the profile of the chairs, making the attached writing tablet and armrest more visible, and showing the thinness of the seat panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backrests and the supporting frames, with the mounting bases clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the other profile of the chairs, with the writing tablet and armrest on the far side, and the flatness of the seat surfaces.
